---
output: github_document
---

<!-- README.md is generated from README.Rmd. Please edit that file -->

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  fig.path = "man/figures/README-",
  out.width = "100%"
)
```

# infvctrs

<!-- badges: start -->
<!-- badges: end -->

The goal of infvctrs is to ...

## Installation

You can install the development version of infvctrs like so:

``` r
# FILL THIS IN! HOW CAN PEOPLE INSTALL YOUR DEV PACKAGE?
```

## Example

Base types of series:

- numeric
- arithmetic (and integers)
  - Difference being tolerance

Transformed series:

- linear
- negated
- inverted


## Similar Packages

- ['peruse'](https://jacgoldsm.github.io/peruse/) provides tools for iterating
  a sequence like with `next_discrete()`. It focuses on fully general sequences
  without transformation.
- ['sets']() package focusses on set operations.
- ['set6']() allows for infinite sets but was removed from CRAN.
- ['Zseq'](https://cran.r-project.org/package=Zseq) provides access to various
  named integer sequences like the Fibonnaci sequence and Prime numbers, but
  only returns finite vectors.
